hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, 2. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
Flop — 2, King, King. Your opponent bet. You called.
River — 8. Your opponent fired a large river bet.

wrongReasons: 7 7 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 7 7 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
Q K makes a three of a kind here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only one pair, 3 6 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 2 J

Your opponent flopped two pair and bet all three streets with growing sizing. The consistent pressure — especially the larger turn bet — signals a hand strong enough to protect but not quite a monster.
